_description: SlashMyBill — Sample Bedrock API Call (Request + Response)

content.text:
You are an AWS billing expert. Analyze this AWS bill and provide insights.

## Bill Data:
Invoice Number: 1234567890
Account ID: 991105135552
Billing Period: 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28
Currency: USD
Total Cost: 2847.53

Service Breakdown:
  - Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud: 1245.67
  - Amazon Simple Storage Service: 312.45
  - Amazon Relational Database Service: 567.89
  - AWS Lambda: 45.23
  - Amazon CloudFront: 89.12
  - Amazon DynamoDB: 34.56
  - Amazon Route 53: 12.50
  - AWS Key Management Service: 8.00
  - Amazon Simple Notification Service: 2.11
  - AmazonCloudWatch: 530.00

Line Item Details (from the bill):
  Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud:
    - USE1-BoxUsage:m5.xlarge: 672 Hrs x $0.192/hr = $129.02
    - USE1-BoxUsage:r5.2xlarge: 744 Hrs x $0.504/hr = $374.98
    - EBS:VolumeUsage.gp2: 2000 GB-Mo x $0.10/GB = $200.00
  Amazon Relational Database Service:
    - USE1-InstanceUsage:db.r5.large: 744 Hrs x $0.25/hr = $186.00

Commitment Discount Status:
  - Savings Plans: NOT DETECTED (customer is likely paying On-Demand rates)
  - Reserved Instances: NOT DETECTED

## Cost Optimization Tips:
- [EC2] Right-size EC2 instances: Use AWS Compute Optimizer to analyze historical utilization and identify underutilized instances. (Estimated savings: 20-40%, Difficulty: easy)
- [EC2] Use Reserved Instances or Savings Plans: For steady-state workloads, commit to 1-year or 3-year Reserved Instances or Savings Plans. (Estimated savings: 30-72%, Difficulty: medium)
- [S3] Use S3 Intelligent-Tiering: Enable for data with unknown or changing access patterns. (Estimated savings: 40-70%, Difficulty: easy)
- [RDS] Right-size RDS instances: Monitor CPU, memory, and I/O utilization. (Estimated savings: 20-50%, Difficulty: medium)
- [General] Compute Savings Plans: Commit to a consistent $/hr spend for 1 or 3 years. (Estimated savings: up to 66%, Difficulty: medium)

## CRITICAL RULES — Savings Plans & Reserved Instances eligibility:
Compute Savings Plans (up to 66% off) apply ONLY to: Amazon EC2, AWS Lambda, AWS Fargate.
EC2 Instance Savings Plans (up to 72% off) apply ONLY to: Amazon EC2.
Database Savings Plans (up to 35% off) apply ONLY to: Amazon RDS, Amazon Aurora, Amazon DynamoDB, Amazon ElastiCache.
Reserved Instances apply ONLY to: Amazon EC2, Amazon RDS, Amazon Redshift, Amazon ElastiCache, Amazon OpenSearch.
DO NOT recommend Savings Plans or Reserved Instances for: VPC, Route 53, CloudWatch, S3, KMS, SNS, SQS, API Gateway, ELB.
CloudFront Security Savings Bundle (up to 30% off): Available for Amazon CloudFront only.

Provide analysis in this JSON format:

1. SUMMARY: 2-3 sentences about total cost and top spenders.
2. SERVICE_ANALYSIS: For each service: explanation, billing_details, recommendations with estimated_savings %.
3. SAVINGS_PLAN_ANALYSIS: Based on Commitment Discount Status.

Respond in this exact JSON:
{
  "summary": "...",
  "service_analysis": [
    {
      "service": "...",
      "cost": "...",
      "explanation": "...",
      "billing_details": "...",
      "recommendations": [{"title": "...", "description": "...", "estimated_savings": "..."}]
    }
  ],
  "savings_plan_analysis": {
    "has_savings_plans": true/false,
    "has_reserved_instances": true/false,
    "recommendation": "...",
    "potential_savings_percent": "...",
    "how_to_purchase": "..."
  }
}

text_parsed.summary: Your February 2026 AWS bill totals $2,847.53 across 10 services. Amazon EC2 is the largest cost driver at $1,245.67 (43.7%), followed by Amazon CloudWatch at $530.00 (18.6%) and Amazon RDS at $567.89 (19.9%). No Savings Plans or Reserved Instances were detected — you are paying full On-Demand rates.

service_analysis.explanation: You ran m5.xlarge instances for 672 hours at $0.192/hr ($129.02) and r5.2xlarge instances for 744 hours at $0.504/hr ($374.98). EBS gp2 storage consumed 2,000 GB-Mo at $0.10/GB ($200.00). All usage is On-Demand with no commitment discounts applied.

recommendations.title: Purchase Compute Savings Plans
recommendations.description: Commit to a $/hr spend for 1 or 3 years to cover EC2 usage. All Upfront 3-year offers the deepest discount.

recommendations.title: Migrate EBS from gp2 to gp3
recommendations.description: gp3 volumes are 20% cheaper than gp2 and offer configurable IOPS/throughput.

service_analysis.explanation: CloudWatch charges include custom metrics, dashboards, log ingestion, and alarms. At $530/month this suggests high-volume log ingestion or many custom metrics.

recommendations.title: Review log retention and ingestion volume
recommendations.description: Reduce log retention periods, filter unnecessary logs at source, and use CloudWatch Logs Insights instead of exporting to S3.

service_analysis.explanation: You ran a db.r5.large instance for 744 hours at $0.25/hr ($186.00). Additional charges include storage, I/O, and backup.

recommendations.title: Purchase RDS Reserved Instance
recommendations.description: For a steady-state database, a 1-year All Upfront RI for db.r5.large saves significantly.

recommendations.title: Consider Aurora Serverless v2
recommendations.description: If workload is variable, Aurora Serverless scales to zero and charges only for capacity used.

service_analysis.explanation: S3 charges include storage, requests, and data transfer. Review storage class distribution and lifecycle policies.

recommendations.title: Enable S3 Intelligent-Tiering
recommendations.description: Automatically moves objects between access tiers based on usage patterns.

service_analysis.explanation: CloudFront charges for data transfer out and HTTP/HTTPS requests.

recommendations.title: CloudFront Security Savings Bundle
recommendations.description: Commit to monthly spend for 1 year for up to 30% savings. Includes free WAF usage.

service_analysis.explanation: Lambda charges based on number of requests and GB-seconds of compute time.

recommendations.title: Right-size Lambda memory
recommendations.description: Use AWS Lambda Power Tuning to find optimal memory. More memory = more CPU = faster execution = potentially lower cost.

service_analysis.explanation: DynamoDB charges for read/write capacity units and storage.

recommendations.title: Switch to On-Demand capacity
recommendations.description: If traffic is unpredictable, On-Demand avoids paying for unused provisioned capacity.

service_analysis.explanation: Route 53 charges $0.50/hosted zone/month plus per-query fees.

recommendations.title: Consolidate hosted zones
recommendations.description: Remove unused hosted zones. Each zone costs $0.50/month.

service_analysis.explanation: Each customer-managed KMS key costs $1/month. You have 8 keys.

recommendations.title: Audit unused KMS keys
recommendations.description: Disable or delete keys not used for encryption/decryption in the past 90 days. Use AWS-managed keys where possible (free).

service_analysis.explanation: SNS charges for published messages and deliveries.

savings_plan_analysis.recommendation: No Savings Plans or Reserved Instances were detected. You are paying On-Demand rates for all services. For your EC2 workloads, a Compute Savings Plan (1-year, All Upfront) could save up to 66%. For RDS, consider a Database Savings Plan or Reserved Instance for up to 60% savings.
savings_plan_analysis.how_to_purchase: Go to AWS Cost Explorer > Savings Plans > Purchase Savings Plans. Choose hourly commitment, term (1 or 3 years), and payment option. For RIs: EC2 Console > Reserved Instances > Purchase.
